Buyer's question

When Should I Hire a Fractional CMO? Between $3M and $30M ARR, with a team to lead.

The right window for a fractional CMO is narrower than most CEOs realize. Too early, and you do not yet have anything to manage; too late, and you need a full-time leader. Here's the timing framework.

Short answer

Hire a fractional CMO when you are between \$3M and \$30M in ARR, you already have at least 1-2 marketing executors, you can name a 12-month outcome, and the CEO will commit 2-3 hours/week to marketing decisions. Below \$3M, founder-led marketing usually wins. Above \$30M, full-time CMO is usually the right next step.

By Bill Colbert · Founder, Treetop Growth Strategy
Published May 2026 · More from the library

The five timing signals

  1. Revenue is between \$3M and \$30M ARR in a B2B model.
  2. You already have 1-2 marketing executors (or one strong marketing manager). A fractional CMO needs people to work through; absent that, hire the executor first.
  3. Founder-led marketing has hit its limit. The CEO can no longer carry it without losing focus on product, sales, or hiring.
  4. You can articulate a 12-month outcome (pipeline growth, brand recognition, conversion improvement).
  5. You can commit \$8K-\$20K/month for 12-18 months — and the CEO has 2-3 hours/week for marketing decisions.

Common premature signals

  • Pre-PMF. If you are still searching for product-market fit, do not scale marketing yet. Fix the product.
  • Below \$3M ARR. Founder voice usually outperforms a fractional CMO at this scale. Use the budget for a strong content marketer or paid media operator instead.
  • Hiring to fix sales. A fractional CMO won't fix a sales problem. Consider a fractional CRO or sales coaching instead.
  • No marketing team yet. Hire the executor first; the strategist later.

Common late signals

  • Above \$30M ARR. You need a full-time CMO at this scale. A fractional can bridge during search, but is not the long-term answer.
  • Five+ marketing people already. A fractional cannot lead a team that big well. Hire full-time.
  • Marketing is the largest line item in your operating budget. Anything that big needs a full-time leader.

The 30-second test

Answer yes or no to all five:

  1. Are you between \$3M and \$30M ARR? (Y/N)
  2. Do you have 1-2 marketing executors already? (Y/N)
  3. Can you describe what "success in 12 months" looks like? (Y/N)
  4. Will the CEO commit 2-3 hours/week to marketing decisions? (Y/N)
  5. Can you commit \$8K-\$20K/month for 12-18 months? (Y/N)

Five yeses = now is the right time. Four yeses = address the gap first. Three or fewer = different solution.

FAQ

How long should a fractional CMO engagement last?

12-18 months is the typical sweet spot. Shorter rarely compounds; longer often means the engagement needed to become a full-time hire.

Should I hire fractional or full-time?

Below \$15M ARR, fractional is usually right. \$15M-\$30M, either works depending on hiring market. Above \$30M, full-time is usually right.

How much does a fractional CMO cost?

\$8K-\$25K/month for a B2B mid-market engagement, depending on time commitment and seniority.

Can a fractional CMO build my team?

Yes. Many great engagements include hiring 1-2 marketers during the engagement. A good fractional knows the talent market.

What if I outgrow the fractional?

Plan for it. A good fractional helps you spec and recruit your full-time CMO when you outgrow the model.
